overview.short: The Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple is a temple dedicated to Venkateshvara, a form of the Hindu deity Vishnu.
overview.description: The Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple is a temple dedicated to Venkateshvara, a form of the Hindu deity Vishnu. It is located at the Indian village of Thirupparkadal at Ranipet district, Tamil Nadu. It is considered one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (79 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Arulmigu Prasanna Venkatesa Perumal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
